analysis.overall_assessment: The image shows a mix of healthy and unhealthy tea leaves, with a majority being healthy. The quality grade is Premium for the healthy leaves.

pollution_reduction_solutions: Implement proper ventilation and air filtration systems to reduce O3 exposure
Regularly monitor air quality levels in the processing area

immediate_actions: Sort out unhealthy leaves from healthy ones
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: Separate leaves based on visible symptoms
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Process healthy leaves separately to maintain quality
quality_preservation: Handle healthy leaves gently to prevent bruising or damage

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: Healthy leaves can be used for tea processing, while unhealthy ones should be discarded carefully
alternative_uses: Unhealthy leaves can be used as compost or mulch
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Compost unhealthy leaves separately from healthy ones to prevent contamination
prevention_measures: Regularly inspect and maintain processing equipment to minimize damage

economic_impact.estimated_loss_percentage: Estimated loss of 10% in product value due to unhealthy leaves
cost_saving_opportunities: Invest in better air filtration systems
value_recovery_methods: Sell unhealthy leaves as compost or mulch
